indices.name: S&P 500
indices.notes: January 2026 ended positive despite daily loss on Jan 30
indices.original_text: The S&P 500 index fell 0.43% on Friday, January 30, 2026, to finish at 6,939.03. Despite this daily loss, the index finished the month of January 2026 with a gain of 1.4%.

indices.name: NASDAQ Composite
indices.notes: Tech stocks under pressure, Microsoft slide
indices.original_text: The Nasdaq composite lost 0.9% on Friday, January 30, 2026, closing at 23,461.82.

indices.name: KOSPI
indices.notes: Record high driven by AI stocks, 4th consecutive session gain. Topped 5,300 intraday.
indices.original_text: The KOSPI index briefly surpassed the 5,300 mark during intraday trading on January 30, 2026, hitting a high of 5,321.68, driven by investor interest in AI shares. The index closed slightly higher at 5,224.3, marking its fourth consecutive session of gains and a new record closing high. KOSPI surged by 22.7% and topped G20 growth.

indices.name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
indices.notes: Yields held steady after Fed decision, slight uptick after Warsh nomination
indices.original_text: The yield on the 10-year note finished January 30, 2026 at 4.26%. The 10-year Treasury yield was more than 1 basis point lower at 4.237% on Thursday, January 29, 2026, as investors weighed the Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady.

indices.name: USD/KRW
indices.notes: Won weakened after dollar strength on Warsh nomination
indices.original_text: The USD to KRW exchange rate on January 30, 2026, was $1 = ₩1,450.79. USD/KRW Open: 1,429.7300, Close: 1,450.2900.

indices.name: Gold (Spot)
indices.notes: Crashed from record high $5,500+ to ~$4,895 on Jan 30. Biggest intraday decline since early 1980s.
indices.original_text: Gold fell more than 12% to slump below $5000 an ounce on Friday, January 30, 2026, marking its biggest intraday decline since the early 1980s. Gold peaked above US$5,500 per ounce on Thursday before dropping to around US$5068 by the end of Friday. Spot gold shed around 9% to trade at $4,895.22 an ounce.

indices.name: Silver (Spot)
indices.notes: Crashed from $120+ to ~$83. Worst day since March 1980. Silver futures settled at $78.53 (-31.4%).
indices.original_text: Silver futures plummeted 31.4% to settle at $78.53, marking its worst day since March 1980. Spot silver was down 28% at $83.45 an ounce. Silver hit more than US$120 per ounce last week before crashing to US$98.50 on Friday.

indices.name: Bitcoin
indices.notes: Crypto market surging with ETF inflows and adoption. Potential short-term pressure if Treasury sell-off continues.
indices.original_text: The crypto market is surging, with Bitcoin crossing $108,000, driven by ETF inflows and adoption. Capital is flowing into crypto ($8B inflow noted). BlackRock has expressed concerns about the sustainability of long-term government bonds, suggesting they are no longer effective portfolio stabilizers. This environment is causing more investors to turn to cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin (BTC) as a hedge against market volatility.

bitcoin_treasury_impact.details: If a genuine U.S. Treasury sell-off accelerates, the immediate impact on Bitcoin is likely to be negative, causing it to drop sharply due to the general 'risk-off' pivot. However, over a longer horizon, Bitcoin could benefit massively if the sell-off leads to global diversification away from the U.S. dollar.
bitcoin_treasury_impact.stablecoin_treasury_link: Stablecoin issuers (like USDT and USDC) are now significant buyers of short-term US Treasuries to back their reserves, deeply linking the crypto market to Treasury dynamics.

kospi_surge.summary: AI boom + market reforms driving historic rally
kospi_surge.details: KOSPI surged 22.7% in January 2026, topping G20 growth. Driven by AI-related stocks (Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix), automotive/robot tech (Hyundai), and President Lee Jae Myung's 'KOSPI 5,000' initiative with reforms to resolve the 'Korea Discount'. The index topped 5,000 for the first time on January 22, 2026.

gold_silver_crash.summary: Record highs followed by historic crash on Fed chair nomination
gold_silver_crash.details: Gold and silver hit record highs (gold $5,500+, silver $120+) in late January 2026 before crashing dramatically on January 30. The crash was triggered by Kevin Warsh's nomination as Fed chair, which eased concerns about central bank independence and caused the dollar to spike. Profit-taking accelerated the selloff.

fed_leadership_change.summary: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh to replace Jerome Powell
fed_leadership_change.details: On January 30, 2026, President Trump nominated Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell as Fed chair when Powell's term ends in May 2026. Warsh, a former Fed governor (2006-2011), is known as a 'hawk' but has recently aligned with calls for lower rates. The nomination triggered a dollar surge and precious metals crash.

market_impact.immediate: Dollar strengthened, gold/silver crashed, Treasury yields slightly higher
market_impact.expected: Potential for more aggressive rate cuts in second half of 2026

fed_leadership_change.policy_outlook: Warsh may favor reducing Fed's balance sheet (upward pressure on long-term rates) but also supports lower short-term rates

verification_details.cross_verification: All major indices verified across 2-3 independent sources
verification_details.original_text_coverage: 100% - all entries include original source text
